cases.description: DAYS takes END_DATE FIRST, then start_date -- the opposite argument order from DAYS360/YEARFRAC. Verified via https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/days-function-57740535-d549-4395-8728-0f07bff0b9df ("DAYS(end_date, start_date)")
Swapping which date is passed first (now the earlier date is 'end_date') flips the sign -- reinforcing that argument order is significant and not auto-corrected
cases.expected_note: Same two dates as DAYS_basic_end_first_argument_order but with roles swapped -> -14

cases.description: Microsoft's own documented example, using text dates parsed as if by DATEVALUE
cases.expected_note: Exact example from the DAYS function documentation
